data.title: Mattel CEO Says No Leadership Gap After Key Executive Exit — Interview
data.description:
(17:40 GMT) *Mattel CEO Says Leadership Evolution Supports Broader Entertainment Strategy
(17:40 GMT) *Mattel CEO Says There Isn't a Leadership Gap
(17:40 GMT) *Mattel Has a Strong and Evolving Bench, CEO Says
(17:40 GMT) Mattel CEO Says No Leadership Gap After Key Executive Exit — Interview

By Connor Hart

Ynon Kreiz said there isn't a leadership gap at Mattel following the surprise departure of a top executive that raised fresh questions about succession planning.

The maker of Barbie dolls and Hot Wheels cars has been actively reshaping its leadership team as part of a larger ambition to evolve from a traditional toy company into a full-scale entertainment powerhouse.

The strategy came into focus earlier this month when Steve Totzke, Mattel's president and chief commercial officer — who was widely viewed as second-in-command and a potential chief executive successor — said he would step down. Totzke will remain with the company through the end of the year in an advisory role.

"It is with great pride, gratitude, and confidence that I am stepping down," Totzke said in a LinkedIn post. He added that will pass the commercial leadership reins to Sanjay Luthra, describing him as "a good friend, an exceptional leader, and I have no doubt he will push us forward in remarkable ways."

Totzke's departure surprised Wall Street and sparked speculation about a possible leadership void. Kreiz rejected that notion, however, pointing instead to what he described as a strong and evolving executive bench.

As part of that effort, Mattel recently elevated Roberto Stanichi to chief global brand officer, giving him oversight of more than 100 brands across the company's portfolio, including Barbie, Hot Wheels, Fisher-Price and Uno.

Stanichi's promotion follows a period of rapid growth at Hot Wheels under his leadership, including expansion into digital content and live events.

Meanwhile, Luthra steps into the chief commercial officer role after more than two decades with the company. Kreiz credited him with transforming Mattel's business in India, navigating complex markets and driving growth through digital marketing and multi-channel expansion.

The executive changes come amid a broader reshuffling at the top. Last year, Paul Ruh succeeded Anthony DiSilvestro as chief financial officer.

The executive changes come as Mattel pivots beyond the traditional toy aisle and ramps up its focus on other entertainment categories, such as films.

The transformation gained momentum with the success of its blockbuster "Barbie" movie in 2023. The company is now doubling down on Hollywood, with upcoming releases including "Masters of the Universe" and "Matchbox: The Movie," which Kreiz has described as "masthead events" for the toymaker's next phase of growth.

The strategy extends beyond the big screen as well, with Mattel also investing across television, gaming, publishing, consumer products and location-based entertainment.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Swiss Market Ends On Firm Note
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - After opening weak and languishing in negative territory till a little before noon, the Switzerland stock market slowly gained in strength on Thursday to eventually end the session on a firm note.

Investors continued to closely follow the developments on the geopolitical front, in addition to tracking the monetary policy moves by the Federal Reserve, European Central Bank and the Bank of England.

The benchmark SMI, which slipped to 12,940.51 in early trades, ended the day with a gain of 104.37 points or 0.8% at 13,136.27 slightly off the session's high of 13,147.61.

Galderma Group climbed nearly 3%. ABB, Novartis, Julius Baer and Sandoz Group gained 2%-2.3%. Richemont and Swisscom moved up 1.75% and 1.61%, respectively.

Swiss Re, Zurich Insurance Group, Lonza Group, Schindler Ps and Swiss Life Holding gained 1%-1.3%.

Amrize dropped 5.77%. Straumann Holding fell 3.3%.

In economic news, Switzerland's KOF Economic Barometer climbed to 97.9 in April, up from a nine-month low of 95.6 in March and exceeding market expectations of 95.9, a report from Swiss Economic Institute (KOF) showed.

data.title: Trane Technologies plc 1Q 2026: Revenue $4.97B, EPS $2.62— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Trane Technologies plc reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue up 6% year over year to $4.97B, while net earnings were $589.5M and diluted earnings per share were $2.62, modestly below the prior-year quarter — a 10-Q summary.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$4.97B$4.69B6%
Net income²$589.5M$609.1M(3.2%)
Diluted EPS³$2.62$2.67(1.9%)

¹ Reported as “Net revenues”. ² Reported as “Net earnings”. ³ Reported as “Diluted earnings per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by higher volume, pricing, acquisitions and favorable foreign exchange.
  • Americas and Asia Pacific showed stronger Commercial HVAC demand, while transport refrigeration softened.
  • Company completed acquisitions including Stellar Energy Americas and the remaining stake in LiquidStack, and took a 49% position in Kieback&Peter, bolstering brand and distribution reach.
  • Gross margin contracted about 100 basis points due to inflationary pressure but was partially offset by productivity and price realization; the company continued to reinvest for growth.

Original SEC Filing: Trane Technologies plc [ TT ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Martin Marietta Materials 1Q 2026: Revenue $1.36B, EPS $25.06— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Martin Marietta Materials reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $1.36B and sharply higher earnings versus the prior-year quarter, driven by volume growth, recent asset additions and specialty product strength.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $1.36B, compared with $1.16B in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 17.2%.
  • Net income was $1.51B, compared with $116M in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 1204%.
  • Diluted EPS was $25.06, compared with $1.9 in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 1220%.

Business Highlights

  • Completed an asset exchange with QUIKRETE on Feb. 23, 2026, adding roughly 20M tons of aggregates capacity across Virginia, Missouri, Kansas and British Columbia while divesting the Midlothian cement plant and Texas ready-mix operations.
  • Total aggregates shipments rose 12.4% to 43.9M tons, with overall revenue growth to $1.362B driven by organic volume increases and contributions from acquired operations.
  • Reportable segments were realigned into East Group and West Group; the West Group now includes a larger mix of ready-mix and asphalt and lower average selling price geographies.
  • Results reflect typical seasonality and weather exposure in the first quarter, with winter slowdowns affecting production, shipments and margins.
  • Specialties revenue increased to $143M and gross profit rose 17%, aided by a 2025 acquisition and pricing gains, partially offset by lower shipments and higher energy costs.

Original SEC Filing: MARTIN MARIETTA MATERIALS INC [ MLM ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares 1Q 2026: Revenue $482M, EPS $0.25— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Huntington Bancshares reported first-quarter 2026 results with net income of $482M and diluted EPS of $0.25, down from $500M and $0.34 in the year-ago quarter, reflecting acquisition-related costs and integration investments following the Cadence and Veritex deals.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$482M$500M(3.6%)
Net income²$482M$500M(3.6%)
Diluted EPS³$0.25$0.34(26.5%)

¹ Reported as “Net income applicable to common shares”. ² Reported as “Net income applicable to common shares”. ³ Reported as “Per common share”.

Business Highlights

  • Net interest income rose 33% year-over-year, driven by asset growth from the Cadence and Veritex acquisitions and higher net interest margin, lifting total FTE revenue.
  • Expanded national commercial and vehicle finance reach; payments, wealth and capital markets fees increased notably.
  • Completed integrations of Cadence and Veritex, adding branches and presence across new states and verticals; incurred approximately $263M of acquisition-related costs.
  • Ramped hiring and increased technology and data investments to scale combined operations, while focusing on organic growth, digital acquisition and risk management, including scenario-driven allowance for credit loss adjustments.

Original SEC Filing: HUNTINGTON BANCSHARES INC /MD/ [ HBAN ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

data.title: Albany International 1Q 2026: Revenue $311.33M, EPS $0.54— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Albany International reported first-quarter 2026 results with consolidated net revenue of $311.33M, up 7.8% from $288.77M a year earlier, while net income attributable to the company was $15.28M and diluted EPS was $0.54.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$311.33M$288.77M7.8%
Net income²$15.28M$17.36M(12%)
Diluted EPS³$0.54$0.56(3.6%)

¹ Reported as “Net revenues”. ² Reported as “Net income attributable to the Company”. ³ Reported as “Earnings per share attributable to Company shareholders - Diluted”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by strong AEC (Advanced Engineered Composites) volume gains despite softness in MC (Machine Clothing).
  • Within MC, publication paper demand declined while Packaging and Tissue grew; Asia was weaker whereas Europe and the Americas were stable.
  • AEC continued momentum on key programs — ramping ASC, GE LEAP and B787 efforts — helping margins and program performance.
  • Operational moves included an AEC strategic review that classified the Salt Lake City facility assets as held-for-sale, and MC actions to consolidate production and optimize its footprint.
  • The company remains focused on investing in AEC 3D‑woven technology and MC manufacturing productivity to support future earnings.

Original SEC Filing: ALBANY INTERNATIONAL CORP /DE/ [ AIN ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

data.title: FCC's Carr Says Disney TV License Review Isn't About Kimmel — WSJ
data.description:
By Joe Flint

FCC Chairman Brendan Carr on Thursday said the decision to order an early license review for Disney's ABC television stations isn't related to President Trump's skirmish with late-night host Jimmy Kimmel.

Rather, he said the review is related to the Federal Communications Commission's investigation into Disney's diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.

"This was based on DEI conduct and not speech," Carr said at a press conference following the FCC's monthly meeting.

Carr's order came the day after Trump demanded Kimmel's firing on Truth Social on Monday for a joke the comedian made at the expense of first lady Melania Trump on ABC's "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" last week.

The timing of Carr's order led many media watchdogs to believe there was a connection and saw it as the latest escalation in the feud between Kimmel and the administration. The FCC's Anna Gomez, the agency's lone Democratic commissioner, this week called Carr's order "the most egregious action this FCC has taken in violation of the First Amendment to date."

The FCC first launched its Disney probe in March 2025 into whether the company was still engaging in DEI practices that run afoul of the regulatory agency's equal employment opportunity rules. He said the information the FCC has received from Disney has led him to suspect the company is in violation of the agency's rules against discrimination based on race and gender.

Carr also suggested Disney hasn't been completely forthcoming in its responses to the FCC's requests for information.

"It felt like they were playing rope-a-dope," Carr said of Disney's response to the agency's document demands.

A Disney spokeswoman didn't immediately respond to a request for comment on Carr's remarks.

Disney previously said it is confident that the "record demonstrates our continued qualifications as licensees under the Communications Act and the First Amendment and are prepared to show that through the appropriate legal channels."

Disney has until May 28 to comply with the FCC's order. It owns eight television stations in major markets including New York and Los Angeles.

On his April 23 show, Kimmel presented a mock White House Correspondents' Dinner speech where he joked that the first lady had "a glow like an expectant widow."

The joke was made days before a man opened fire outside the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner. The suspected gunman, Cole Allen, was charged earlier this week with attempting to assassinate Trump and two firearm offenses.

Trump on Thursday restated his demand that Kimmel be fired. Kimmel has continued his jokes at the president's expense in his late night monologues.

Write to Joe Flint at Joe.Flint@wsj.com

data.title: Production Dead, Demand Alive: Used Tesla Model X Is Flying Off Lots
data.description:
Key points:
  • Tesla’s decision to retire the two vehicles came amid falling demand; sales of the Model X, S, and Cybertruck vehicles cumulatively accounted for just 3% of Tesla’s overall global deliveries in 2025. 
  • In the U.S., the Model X is priced at around $100,000, compared to Tesla’s Model Y mass market SUV, which starts at about $40,000. 
  • According to a study from iSeeCars.com, the demand for used Tesla Model X vehicles remains exceptionally strong even as the broader used-car market slows and non-Tesla electric vehicles struggle with plunging prices and longer selling times.

Demand for Tesla Inc’s (TSLA) Model X SUV, slated to end production this quarter, is fast rising in the used vehicle market, according to a new study.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk first announced the company’s decision to retire the Model S and X in January. Earlier this month, the CEO said during Tesla’s first-quarter earnings call that the last of the vehicles would be produced at the company’s factory in California in early May.

Both the Model S sports sedan and the Model X SUV are Tesla’s more premium vehicles. In the U.S., the Model X is priced at around $100,000, compared to Tesla’s Model Y mass market SUV, which starts at about $40,000.

Tesla’s decision to retire the two vehicles came amid falling demand; sales of the Model X, S, and Cybertruck vehicles collectively accounted for just 3% of Tesla’s overall global deliveries in 2025.

What Does The Study Show?

The demand for used Tesla Model X vehicles remains exceptionally strong, even as the broader used-car market slows and non-Tesla electric vehicles struggle with plunging prices and longer selling times.

A study from iSeeCars.com, which examined 1- to 5-year-old used cars sold in the first quarters of 2025 and 2026, found the Tesla Model X ranked No. 1 among the 20 fastest-selling used cars. On average, listed Model X vehicles spent just 25.6 days on the market — roughly 0.4 times the overall market average of 58.8 days and far faster than the 60.1 days required for non-Tesla EVs.

Three of the top 10 fastest-selling models were Teslas, highlighting the brand’s outsized pull among used-car shoppers. While Tesla Cybertruck occupied the 7th position on the list, the Model Y is the 9th fastest-selling used car, the study noted.

Tesla EVs maintained a flat 1.3% share of the 1- to 5-year-old used market in Q1 2026, even as non-Tesla EV share climbed to 2.4%. Average listing prices for Tesla EVs were essentially unchanged year-over-year, dipping a negligible 0.1% to $31,458. By contrast, non-Tesla EV prices fell 10.3% — one of the steepest drops across all powertrain categories.

Selling velocity tells a similar story. Tesla EVs moved in an average of 32.4 days during Q1 2026 — the fastest of any major drivetrain group and a full 27.7 days quicker than non-Tesla EVs. The entire market slowed slightly, but Tesla vehicles continued to clear dealer lots and private listings.

How Did TSLA Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment fell from ‘extremely bullish’ to ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume reduced from ‘high’ to ‘low’ levels.

TSLA stock rose 34% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Coinbase’s ‘CUSHY’ stablecoin fund to launch tokenized share class via Superstate in Q2
data.description:
Coinbase and Superstate are partnering to launch the Coinbase Stablecoin Yield Fund (CUSHY) stablecoin credit offering, which will provide institutional investors with exposure to credit strategies within the stablecoin ecosystem through a structured fund vehicle.

CUSHY expects to launch in the second quarter as the first external fund issued using Superstate’s new FundOS platform, according to the announcement. The fund will be administered by Northern Trust Hedge Fund Services and powered by the Omnium platform.

FundOS is a turnkey operating system for asset managers looking to tokenize fund shares on Solana, Ethereum, and, soon, Base. This theoretically allows shares to be used onchain, like deployed as collateral in a DeFi lending protocol, and be available for 24/7 trading.

Coinbase said its tokenized share class for CUSHY may be made available for collateralization and transfer "across compliant digital venues." The fund itself aims to generate yield by lending stablecoins and unspecified private credit opportunities.

"With CUSHY, we are fusing the high-velocity efficiency of digital rails with the institutional rigor of traditional credit," Coinbase Asset Management President Anthony Bassili said. "We're excited to have major partners such as Northern Trust, Apollo, Superstate, Solana, Base, and Coinbase supporting this important mission."

Late last year, Coinbase Asset Management introduced the Bitcoin Yield Fund (USCBYF) to accredited U.S. investors. The fund is a long-BTC strategy that aims to deliver bitcoin’s performance plus additional yield using private credit lending and basis trading. Last month, Coinbase tapped Apex Group to provide a tokenized share class on Base for the fund.

CUSHY represents the first third-party external fund to tap FundOS, which has so far been used to issue Superstate’s USTB and USCC fund strategies with a combined $1 billion-plus in AUM.

USTB, or Superstate's Short Duration US Government Securities Fund, invests in short-term U.S. Treasurys, while USCC is Superstate's Crypto Carry Fund, which provides exposure to crypto basis trades. Both are limited to qualified purchasers and offer tokenized share classes for onchain ownership and transfer.

Superstate, founded in 2022, is a Securities and Exchange Commission-registered transfer agent that builds tokenized investment products and infrastructure, including its Opening Bell platform used for issuing onchain equities.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: Trump Set to Sign Plan to Expand Retirement Access. It Won't Do Much Without Congress. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Light

President Donald Trump plans to sign an executive order later today meant to encourage more Americans to save for retirement. But the plan is a step down from what was originally proposed.

The order will expand access to retirement accounts for Americans who don't have one with their employer, a White House official said. Some 42% percent of the nation's full-time private sector workforce lacks access to an employer-provided retirement plan, according to the Economic Innovation Group, a bipartisan public policy organization.

The administration's efforts will include creating a new website, TrumpIRA.gov, that helps people find privately run individual retirement accounts that they can sign up for. Trump will also tell the Treasury Department to help bring awareness to the Saver's Match, part of a 2022 law that directs the government to match up to $1,000 in retirement contributions for workers making less than $35,000 a year. The match goes into effect in January. Semafor earlier reported the details of the order.

The order won't be nearly as ambitious as an idea Trump proposed during February's State of the Union address. During that speech, Trump said his administration planned to give "access to the same type of retirement plan offered to every federal worker" to Americans without an employer-sponsored plan. Federal employees have access to the Thrift Savings Plan, which includes a government match and very low-cost index funds.

Giving Americans access to the TSP would likely require legislation. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent at the time suggested Congress could enact the idea through reconciliation, a process lawmakers used to pass a tax and spending law last year and avoid the filibuster. Legislation introduced by lawmakers seeking to expand the TSP hasn't advanced.

Another idea floated in February was that the White House would revive myRA accounts, an IRA-like savings vehicle launched by former President Barack Obama. Savers in those accounts were limited to investing in Treasury savings bonds, and the first Trump White House closed the program after it saw very little demand.

The White House hopes the new effort will see some of the same momentum as Trump Accounts, the new savings vehicles for children established by last year's tax law. In early April, the Treasury Department said five million children had already been signed up for the accounts, which include a $1,000 contribution from the government for U.S. citizens born between 2025 and 2028.

Some private-sector donors have said they'll make contributions to Trump Accounts, and donors could do the same for older savers in conjunction with the new retirement account push.

That the White House will help savers find privately run IRAs — rather than try to expand ones of its own — will be cheered by the investment industry, which has pushed back on proposals to expand government-run accounts.

After the State of the Union, the Investment Company Institute, which represents the asset management industry, released a statement saying the retirement system shouldn't shift "to a government-run one-size-fits-all approach."

Write to Joe Light at joe.light@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: CDC Says E. Coli Outbreak Linked to Raw Farm LLC Products Is Over
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said an E. coli outbreak linked to Raw Farm LLC products is over and the Food and Drug Administration said it ended its investigation.

The CDC said there were nine reported illnesses with the outbreak strain, including three hospitalizations, in line with the totals reported in a March 25 update.

The CDC that out of eight people interviewed, seven reported consumption of Raw Farm brand dairy products including cheddar cheese and milk.

In March, the CDC said it was investigating E. coli infections linked to Raw Farm cheddar cheese, and the Fresno, Calif., company said, "We disagree 100% with the allegations made by the FDA and CDC." The company said, "All our milk is tested prior to making cheese and our finished cheese is tested prior to release. We have no customer complaints at this time."

The FDA said April 16 that it collected and analyzed 19 samples of Raw Farm LLC raw cheddar cheese and one sample tested positive for E. coli. It said analysis through whole-genome sequencing showed that the E. coli strain found in the cheese was a different strain from that of the recent outbreak.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: US crude oil output rose to two-month high in February, EIA says
data.description:
U.S. field production of crude oil rose to 13.63 million barrels per day in February, the highest since December, data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration showed on Thursday.

U.S. oil production in February was up both month-on-month and year-over-year. Output averaged 13.24 million bpd in January this year and February last year, the EIA data showed.

U.S. oil demand also rose in February, led by a multi-year jump in demand for distillate fuel oils, which include diesel and heating oil, the EIA data showed.

Product supplied of crude oil and petroleum products, the EIA's proxy for demand, averaged 21.14 million bpd in February, up from 20.65 million bpd in January and the highest since August last year, the data showed.

Demand for distillate fuel oils rose from about 4 million bpd in January to 4.21 million bpd in February, the highest since February 2022, the EIA data showed.

Meanwhile, U.S. gross natural gas production from the Lower 48 states rose to 134.0 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) in February, up from 132.0 bcfd in January but down from a record 136.0 bcfd in December.

In top gas-producing states, monthly output in February rose by 2.8% to 38.3 bcfd in Texas and 0.4% to 21.4 bcfd in Pennsylvania, the EIA said.

That compares with a monthly all-time high of 38.7 bcfd in December 2025 in Texas and 21.9 bcfd in December 2021 in Pennsylvania.

data.title: Builders FirstSource lowers 2026 sales forecast on weak housing market
data.description:
Construction materials supplier Builders FirstSource lowered its 2026 revenue estimates amid a weak residential market, sending its shares down almost 6%.

The company, which supplies building materials and products for home construction, remains under pressure as housing demand has stayed subdued due to affordability constraints amid higher prices and interest rates.

  • The firm also authorized additional share repurchases worth $300 million, bringing the total, including its prior April 2025 authorization, to $500 million

  • It posted first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of 27 cents compared with analysts' estimate of 37 cents, as per data compiled by LSEG.

  • Net sales stood at $3.29 billion, down 10% from last year.

  • For 2026, the company expects its net sales to be in a range of $14.6 billion to $15.6 billion, compared to previous expectations of $14.8 billion to $15.8 billion.

data.title: Soy basis steady-firmer at crushing sites
data.description:
Spot basis bids for soybeans firmed at several soy crushing facilities in the U.S. Midwest on Thursday, reflecting processor demand for soybean supplies as crush margins remain elevated.

  • The spot soy basis firmed by 5 to 10 cents a bushel at crushing plants in Decatur, Indiana; Lafayette, Indiana, and Council Bluffs, Iowa.

  • Basis bids for corn were mostly steady, with only a few mixed changes.

  • More locations have rolled their basis bids to the Chicago Board of Trade July corn (CN26), soybean (SN26) and wheat (WN26) futures contracts as the CBOT's May contracts approach their May 14 expiration.

  • On the CBOT, July corn (CN26) was down 2-3/4 cents at $4.65 a bushel near midday on Thursday while July soybeans (SN26) were up 1/4 cent at $11.97-1/4 a bushel.
